mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.50% and remains there for at least one month, equity risk premiums compress as the cost of capital declines. This monetary easing signal produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors rebalance into equities.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS=0.16% indicates deep easing regime
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with near-zero rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during easing regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth above 100,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum and consumer income expansion, driving positive equity returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS growth and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or correlation becomes negative in more than 40% of rolling 6-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts below 500,000 units signals weakening demand and future economic contraction. This demand shock produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward earnings expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST drops below 500,000 units in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows zero predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises above 9.5%, signaling stagflationary pressure. This joint deterioration in supply capacity and labor slack produces negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: stagflationary regime with supply constraints and labor slack
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO growth × UNRATE level) shows Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window, or the mechanism fails to outperform single-variable models in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth accelerates above 0.8% month-over-month, indicating strong consumer demand and forward earnings visibility. This demand acceleration produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as consensus earnings estimates are raised.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS growth and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises more than 0.4% month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, real rates turn deeply negative. This negative real rate regime produces positive equity returns over 60 trading days as investors flee cash and seek inflation hedges in equities.
regime_hint: negative real rates with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: The interaction signal (inflation rate × negative real rate spread) shows Rank IC < 0.07 over any 3-year rolling window, or produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points in a single month, signaling an abrupt deterioration in labor market conditions. This shock produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as forward earnings are revised downward due to reduced consumer spending capacity.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or shock period
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized while real retail sales simultaneously contract month-over-month. This joint demand collapse signals recession risk and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity risk premiums widen.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 1.5% annualized AND RSAFS MoM growth < 0%
failure_condition: The interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.08 over any 2-year rolling window, or the mechanism fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances during actual recession periods.
